candidates.title: The Absorption Tax: How Feature Hierarchy Structure Governs SAE Failure Modes Across Semantic Domains
candidates.summary: Evidence-grounded revision of the Absorption Tax proposal. PRIMARY: First systematic cross-domain absorption characterization with the striking pilot finding that first-letter spelling shows the LOWEST absorption (3.9%), while semantic hierarchies show substantially higher rates (city-language 10.4%, city-continent up to 53.4%). SECONDARY: Causal absorption confirmation via activation patching (14.3% recovery vs 0.5% control) and tightened hedging classification (strict 7.4% vs loose 92.6%). TERTIARY: Absorption Tax theorem (qualitative only -- quantitative predictions not supported by pilot data). NEGATIVE RESULTS: GAS unsupervised detector fails (rho=0.12), CMI at L0=22 not supported (rho=0.044), absorption tax quantitative predictions weak. Paper restructured from three-contribution to two-contribution based on evidence.
hypotheses: H1: Cross-domain absorption rates differ significantly from first-letter (PARTIALLY SUPPORTED, p=0.005 for city-language)
H2': Semantic hierarchies show HIGHER absorption than syntactic first-letter (SUPPORTED by pilot, inverting original H2)
H3: Absorption-hedging ratio varies by hierarchy type (SUPPORTED: 69/31 for city-continent vs 45/55 for first-letter)
H5: Absorption tax quantitative predictions (NOT SUPPORTED: rho=0.08 for absorption-MSE)
H6: Architecture ranking consistent across hierarchies (INCONCLUSIVE: JumpReLU consistently best, Matryoshka mixed)
H7: Causal absorption via activation patching (SUPPORTED: 14.3% recovery vs 0.5% control, needs larger n)
candidates.pilot_focus: COMPLETED. 11 of 13 tasks done. Key findings: H2 falsified (first-letter NOT worst case), H4 refuted (GAS fails), H5 not supported. Three blocking experiments completed.
candidates.key_novelty: Cross-domain characterization (9/10, cleanest novelty). Causal absorption via activation patching (8/10, first interventional evidence). Tightened hedging classification (8/10, methodological contribution). Absorption Tax (7/10, qualitative only).
candidates.crowded_risks: SAEBench evaluates absorption but only on first-letter. No competing cross-domain work found as of April 2026. GAS failure means no unsupervised detection claim. Field velocity remains high for architectural mitigations but not for characterization.
candidates.compute_estimate: ~9 GPU-hours total. Phase 1 (cross-domain + probes): 6 hr. Phase 2 (activation patching): 1 hr. Phase 3 (tax validation): 0.5 hr. Phase 4 (negative results): 1 hr CPU.
candidates.falsification_conditions: Comprehensive falsification: probe quality improvement reverses H2' finding (first-letter becomes highest absorption) AND activation patching effect disappears at larger n. Both would need to occur simultaneously to invalidate core claims.
candidates.blocking_dependency: Probe quality improvement at layers 18/24 for RAVEL hierarchies. If all probes remain below F1=0.85, fall back to GPT-2 Small.
candidates.venue_target: Primary: NeurIPS 2026 / ICLR 2027 main. Fallback: EMNLP 2026 / NeurIPS MI Workshop.
key_changes_from_prior_round: Paper restructured from THREE to TWO primary contributions based on pilot evidence
H2 falsified: first-letter is LOWEST absorption, not highest. H2' replaces H2.
GAS demoted to negative result (rho=0.12). No longer claimed as contribution.
CMI demoted to appendix (rho=0.044, p=0.83). No longer theoretical pillar.
Absorption Tax downgraded to qualitative framework (quantitative predictions weak).
H7 (causal absorption via activation patching) added as new secondary contribution.
JumpReLU elevated as consistently best architecture (not Matryoshka).
Tightened hedging classification elevated as methodological contribution.
Experiment-first ordering: all 11 pilot tasks completed before this synthesis.

candidates.title: Controlled Dictionary Experiment: Separating Encoder from Dictionary in Absorption Causation
candidates.summary: Hold decoder dictionary constant, vary encoder (feedforward vs. OMP vs. 2-pass). Tests whether absorption is encoder or dictionary failure. Strengthened by pilot activation patching result confirming encoder-level competitive exclusion.
hypotheses: If amortization gap dominates: OMP absorption < 50% of feedforward at matched L0
If sparsity landscape dominates: absorption similar across encoding methods
candidates.pilot_focus: Feedforward vs. OMP comparison on 1000 tokens, Gemma Scope L12-16k. 30 min.
candidates.key_novelty: 9/10. First controlled dictionary experiment. MP-SAE does not hold dictionary constant.
candidates.pivot_trigger: Activate if cross-domain probes fail AND activation patching effect disappears. Always valuable as supplementary.

candidates.title: Feature Absorption as Competitive Exclusion: Phase Transitions and Limiting Similarity
candidates.summary: Formalize absorption through ecological Lotka-Volterra competition. STRENGTHENED by pilot evidence: cross-domain data shows hierarchy-dependent absorption rates, exactly what the ecological model predicts (different hierarchies = different niche overlap distributions). Predict sharp phase transition at critical cosine similarity theta*.
hypotheses: Phase transition: absorption probability sigmoid with transition width < 0.1
Hierarchy-dependent theta*: first-letter < city-language < city-continent (matching pilot ordering)
candidates.pilot_focus: Plot absorption probability vs. decoder cosine similarity using Phase 1 data. 15 min CPU.
candidates.key_novelty: 9/10. Zero prior work on competitive exclusion in SAE context.
candidates.pivot_trigger: Activate as supplementary analysis when Phase 1 data is available. Provides theoretical explanation for WHY cross-domain rates differ.

candidates.title: Absorption-Aware Post-Hoc Feature Correction
candidates.summary: Given known absorption patterns, construct corrected feature activations by re-activating absorbed parent features when child fires. Tests whether absorption can be handled at inference time without architectural changes. From contrarian perspective's constructive proposal.
hypotheses: Post-hoc correction recovers >= 50% of SAE-vs-probe gap on downstream tasks
Or: correction has negligible effect because absorption permanently rewires decoder direction
candidates.pilot_focus: Apply correction to first-letter absorption using known absorption patterns. 30 min.
candidates.key_novelty: 8/10. First evaluation of post-hoc absorption correction.
candidates.pivot_trigger: Activate if reviewers ask for prescriptive guidance.

dropped_candidates.title: GAS as Primary Unsupervised Absorption Detector
dropped_candidates.reason_dropped: REFUTED by pilot: Spearman rho=0.12 (target 0.6, failure threshold 0.3). Decoder-activation co-occurrence mismatch does not capture absorption. Retained as negative result in appendix.

dropped_candidates.title: Information-Theoretic Absorption Taxonomy via CMI
dropped_candidates.reason_dropped: NOT SUPPORTED by pilot: rho=0.044 (p=0.83) at L0=22. CMI does not predict absorption. Demoted to appendix.

dropped_candidates.title: Absorption Tax with Quantitative Predictions (T(G), R_pc)
dropped_candidates.reason_dropped: Quantitative predictions not supported: absorption-MSE rho=0.08 (p=0.87), R_pc rho=0.16 (p=0.46). Retained as qualitative framework only.

dropped_candidates.title: EDA as Universal Weight-Only Absorption Detector
dropped_candidates.reason_dropped: Works in specific regimes (mid-layer, narrow SAEs) but fails for 75% early absorption. Replaced by GAS, which also failed. Both documented as negative results.

dropped_candidates.title: ITAC as Primary Absorption Correction Method
dropped_candidates.reason_dropped: ITAC achieves 3% vs. 20% target. Structural limitation: 75% early absorption is ineligible.

dropped_candidates.title: Hierarchy-Coherent SAE Loss
dropped_candidates.reason_dropped: Crowded by Muchane et al., Luo et al. HSAE, KronSAE. Requires SAE retraining.

dropped_candidates.title: Absorption Scaling Laws: Parametric Predictions from SAEBench Grid
dropped_candidates.reason_dropped: Novelty 5/10. Quantitative fit is new but qualitative trends known. May be incorporated as supplementary analysis if Phase 1 data warrants it.
